title: cephalaria uralensis var. puberula
intro: Cephalaria uralensis var. puberula, also known as hairy cephalaria or mountain cephalaria, is a plant species within the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Russia and Eastern Europe, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Cephalaria uralensis var. puberula can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs for enhanced medicinal effects.
